title: The fifteen-minute crossing
summary: On the Istanbul ferry, the captain and a daily commuter realise they have shared the same short journey twice a day for thirty years.

body.speaker: Yusuf
body.text: Captain, you do not know me. I want to tell you something.

body.speaker: Captain Emin
body.text: I know you a little. You take this ferry every morning. You wear a brown coat in winter.

body.speaker: Yusuf
body.text: Yes. I have taken this ferry for thirty years. Asian side to European side. Fifteen minutes each way.

body.speaker: Captain Emin
body.text: I have been the captain for thirty-two years. We have been together a long time. We just never spoke.

body.speaker: Yusuf
body.text: Sometimes I think the ferry is a small room. Not a boat. A room that moves between two parts of my life. Work. Home. Work. Home.

body.speaker: Captain Emin
body.text: That is a nice idea. For me, the ferry is the other thing. It is the only part of the city that is not the city. Out here, the water is the same as it was in 1980. The buildings change. The water does not.

body.speaker: Captain Emin
body.text: Most people read on the boat. Or look at the phone. Few people look at the water.

body.speaker: Yusuf
body.text: Today I do not have my book. So I came to the front. So I saw you.

body.speaker: Captain Emin
body.text: Tomorrow you will bring your book again. That is okay. But maybe on Friday, leave the book. Look at the water.

body.speaker: Yusuf
body.text: On Friday I will. I retire in two months. I want to see what I have been crossing.

body.speaker: Captain Emin
body.text: Then we have eight weeks. I will tell you something each Friday. About the water. About the boat. There is more to know than you think.

annotations.meaningVi: phà / thuyền chở khách qua sông biển
phía châu Á (Istanbul nằm trên hai châu lục)
ít người (mang nghĩa âm: không nhiều)
hơn anh nghĩ (cụm so sánh kết câu)

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for ongoing duration: 'have/has + V-ed for/since'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Diễn tả việc bắt đầu trong quá khứ, kéo dài tới hiện tại: 'I have taken this ferry for thirty years.' 'I have been the captain for thirty-two years.' 'We have been together a long time.' Dùng 'for + khoảng thời gian' (for thirty years) hoặc 'since + mốc' (since 1995). Sau have/has dùng quá khứ phân từ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Present perfect describes an action begun in the past and continuing now: 'I have taken this ferry for thirty years.' 'I have been the captain for thirty-two years.' Use 'for + duration' (for thirty years) or 'since + a point in time' (since 1995). The verb after have/has is a past participle.

translationVi:
Yusuf: Thuyền trưởng, ông không biết tôi đâu. Tôi muốn nói với ông một chuyện.
Thuyền trưởng Emin: Tôi biết anh chút ít. Anh đi phà này mỗi sáng. Mùa đông anh mặc áo khoác nâu.
Yusuf: Vâng. Tôi đi phà này ba mươi năm rồi. Từ phía châu Á sang phía châu Âu. Mười lăm phút một chiều.
Thuyền trưởng Emin: Tôi làm thuyền trưởng ba mươi hai năm. Chúng ta đã ở cùng nhau lâu rồi. Chỉ là chưa nói chuyện.
Yusuf: Đôi khi tôi nghĩ phà là một căn phòng nhỏ. Không phải thuyền. Một căn phòng di chuyển giữa hai phần đời tôi. Việc. Nhà. Việc. Nhà.
Thuyền trưởng Emin: Ý hay đấy. Với tôi, phà là chuyện khác. Đó là phần duy nhất của thành phố mà không phải là thành phố. Ngoài này, mặt nước vẫn như năm 1980. Các tòa nhà thay đổi. Nước thì không.
Yusuf: Tôi chưa bao giờ nghĩ thế.
Thuyền trưởng Emin: Phần lớn người ta đọc sách trên phà. Hoặc nhìn điện thoại. Ít người nhìn mặt nước.
Yusuf: Hôm nay tôi không mang sách. Nên tôi đi ra mũi. Nên tôi gặp ông.
Thuyền trưởng Emin: Mai anh lại mang sách. Không sao. Nhưng có lẽ thứ Sáu thì bỏ sách. Nhìn nước đi.
Yusuf: Thứ Sáu tôi sẽ làm vậy. Hai tháng nữa tôi nghỉ hưu. Tôi muốn xem mình đã đi qua cái gì.
Thuyền trưởng Emin: Vậy chúng ta có tám tuần. Mỗi thứ Sáu tôi sẽ kể anh nghe một điều. Về nước. Về phà. Có nhiều thứ để biết hơn anh nghĩ.

questions.prompt: How long has Yusuf taken this ferry?
questions.explanation: 'I have taken this ferry for thirty years.'

questions.prompt: How long does the crossing take each way?
How long has Captain Emin been the captain?
questions.explanation: 'I have been the captain for thirty-two years.'

questions.prompt: How does Yusuf describe the ferry?
options: A small room that moves between two parts of his life
A noisy place full of strangers
questions.explanation: 'The ferry is a small room. Not a boat. A room that moves between two parts of my life.'

questions.prompt: How does Captain Emin describe the ferry?
options: The only part of the city that is not the city — the water has not changed since 1980
A copy of all other ferries

questions.prompt: What do most people do on the ferry?
options: Read or look at their phones
questions.explanation: 'Most people read on the boat. Or look at the phone.'

questions.prompt: Why did Yusuf come to the front today?
options: He did not have his book
questions.explanation: 'Today I do not have my book. So I came to the front.'

options: To tell Yusuf something about the water and the boat each Friday for eight weeks
To give Yusuf a free ticket
To stop the ferry one day
questions.explanation: 'I will tell you something each Friday. About the water. About the boat.'

options: A daily routine can hide a parallel world that only opens when you finally look up from your book
Istanbul should have a bridge instead
questions.explanation: The dialogue's whole arc is about this awakening.
Vocabulary in context: Yusuf retires in two months.

blanks.explanation: The only part of the city that is not the city.
More to know than you think.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Yusuf says, 'I want to see what I have been crossing.' Think of one fifteen-minute moment in your own day that you usually fill with a phone or a book. What might you see if, for one week, you looked up instead?
